_comment: Fixed 2026-07-19: (1) weighted_list is now DERIVED from each pin's own 'weight' field in the shard files (source of truth), not hand-maintained -- previously listings 30-33 (20 pins) were silently missing their 3x rotation boost, a latent bug invisible because nothing had ever run the scheduler. (2) weighted_list is built as successive PASSES over the pool (not grouped repeats) so a stateless day-index walk never lands on the same image on consecutive days -- the original grouped-repeat construction would have posted the identical image 3 days running for every weight-3 pin, defeating the whole point of the fresh-pin variant system. See tools/pick_daily_pin.py. (3) buffer_board_map/buffer_pin_subprofile removed: vestigial from an earlier design, unused by the live pinterest_create_pin posting pipeline. | UPDATED 2026-07-19 (fold-in-the-12 project): added shards 10-11 (60 pin entries: 12 new holiday/back-to-school SKUs x 5 variants each), all weight 3 (newest-listings boost, same pattern as listings 21-34). v1 entries for these 12 reference their already-live Pinterest CDN image directly via an optional per-pin 'image_url' field rather than a repo-hosted file -- see pick_daily_pin.py. weighted_list regenerated accordingly (now length 490, was 310). | UPDATED 2026-07-19 (3rd round, scaling 1->3 posts/day): Bug 3 found & fixed -- the file-level 'successive passes' weighted_list (correct for 1 post/day) breaks the instant you read 3 consecutive slots in a day, because a listing's 5 variant files sit consecutively in the pool -- 3 consecutive slots landed on 3 variants of the SAME listing, not 3 different products. Fixed by rebuilding weighted_list at the LISTING level (pass/weight logic now groups by _base_pin, not by file), with variant cycling layered on top so repeat occurrences of a listing still get a fresh image. Verified via 400-day x 3-slot simulation: zero same-day listing collisions, zero same-day file collisions, zero adjacent-slot file repeats, 32-day minimum gap before any exact file repeats, ~33-day cycle for weight-1 listings and ~8-13 day cycle for weight-3 listings.
weighted_list_derived_from: listing-level pass weighting (grouped by each pin's _base_pin / file stem), with each successive occurrence of a listing assigned its next variant file in v1->v2->v3->v4->v5->v1... order -- regenerate via tools/pick_daily_pin.py:build_weighted_list() any time weights change, do not hand-edit this array. NOTE: this array's length (98) is now a LISTING-level rotation length, not a per-file length -- do not confuse with pool_size (230, the file count).
shard_size: variable (17 for shards 00-09, 30 for shards 10-11 -- not uniform since 2026-07-19)
rotation_algorithm: global_index = day_index * posts_per_day + slot (slot is 0/1/2, one per scheduled trigger); selection_index = global_index % len(weighted_list); pin = pool[weighted_list[selection_index]]. weighted_list operates at the LISTING level (see weighted_list_derived_from) so any 3 consecutive slots always land on 3 different listings. Stateless by design -- same (date, slot) always yields the same pin (safe to re-fire). Implemented in tools/pick_daily_pin.py.
